page: UpCloud HIMEM-4xCPU-32GB is a memory-optimized server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 4 vCPUs and 4 physical cores operating at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor includes 16 MB of L3 cache. The server is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 100 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate average performance in memory read bandwidth, with small block cached reads reaching 415.18 GB/sec and large block uncached reads at 41.36 GB/sec. However, large block write bandwidth is weaker at 19.09 GB/sec. CPU performance is generally modest; the single-core stress-ng score falls in the bottom 10 percent, and the multi-core score is in the bottom 25 percent. Geekbench testing shows average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core performance. LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models but poor for medium 7B models.

From a resource density perspective, this instance offers a high memory capacity relative to its compute resources, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense CPU performance. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and the lower-tier processing speeds, as evidenced by weak database operations and redis SET performance.

This server is suited for memory-intensive applications with moderate compute requirements. Appropriate workloads include caching layers, lightweight database hosting, text processing, and running small-scale language models. It is less suitable for heavy multi-threaded compilation, intensive ray tracing, or high-throughput web serving.
description: The UpCloud HIMEM-4xCPU-32GB is a memory-optimized server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It is configured with 4 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 100 GB local SSD. Performance benchmarks show average memory read bandwidth alongside modest CPU capabilities, with weaker multi-core processing and database throughput. This configuration presents a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand sustained, high-performance compute resources. It is suited for caching systems, text processing, lightweight databases, and small-scale language model inference.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-CPU ratio and bundled SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 4 shared vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 100 GB local SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Average memory read bandwidth up to 415.18 GB/sec
Suited for caching, text processing, and lightweight databases
